_source: Behavioral differences between TS source and Go implementation, extracted by comparing code
_purpose: TDD test targets — each gap is a test that should FAIL until Go is fixed to match TS

continuation_message.ts_message: Output token limit hit. Resume directly — no apology, no recap of what you were doing. Pick up mid-thought if that is where the cut happened. Break remaining work into smaller pieces.
continuation_message.go_message: Please continue from where you left off.
continuation_message.gap: Go uses a generic message, TS uses a specific directive that prevents the model from apologizing or recapping

retry_constants.gap: Go retries only 3 times for all errors. TS retries up to 10 times for general errors and 3 times specifically for 529 overloaded.

error_529_overloaded.gap: Go does not handle 529 overloaded errors at all. TS has specific 529 handling with foreground-only retry policy.

context_too_long_detection.gap: Go only checks for 'context_too_long', TS also checks for 'prompt is too long'

micro_compact_threshold.gap: Go uses a fixed 10KB threshold. TS threshold varies based on tool type and caching state.

compact_session_strategy.go_strategy: keep first + last 2 messages, drop middle
compact_session_strategy.gap: Go uses naive truncation. TS uses LLM to summarize compacted context.

missing_tool_result_synthesis.ts_placeholder: [Tool result missing due to internal error]
missing_tool_result_synthesis.gap: Go does not synthesize missing tool results. TS creates synthetic tool_result blocks for any tool_use that lacks a result.

tool_use_detection.gap: Both use content blocks — Go matches TS here (no gap)

permission_recreation.gap: Go recreates the permission policy every turn. TS shares context across the session.
